title: Default threshold misses ordinary 10 percent regressions

evidence.quote:
change_percent = (current_ns - baseline_ns) / baseline_ns

if change_percent > threshold:

reasoning: The default threshold value is 1.1, which reads as a 1.1x tolerance, but the code compares it to a fractional percent change. A 20% slowdown produces change_percent=0.20 and passes because 0.20 is not greater than 1.1; only slowdowns above 110% are flagged.
recommendation: Either treat threshold as a ratio and compare current_ns > baseline_ns * threshold, or change the default/API to use fractional deltas such as 0.10 for 10%. Clarify the printed threshold text to match the chosen contract.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests exercise boundary cases around the default threshold or a user-supplied threshold.
suggestedRegressionTest: Compare a baseline of 100 ns/op with current 120 ns/op using the default threshold and assert the script reports a regression if the intended default is 10%.
minimumFixScope: Threshold comparison logic and CLI/help text in both scripts.
